1. Skill Coverage Comprehensiveness (30.1a_covered: SQL_basics, SQL_advanced, Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, Seaborn, statistics, machine_learning
1. Skill Coverage Comprehensiveness (30.1b_comment: Only Week 1 SQL is fully visible and it is systematically sequenced (SELECT/filtering -> JOIN -> GROUP BY/HAVING -> subqueries/CTEs -> window functions -> practice project). However, important advanced/interview-relevant SQL areas are not explicitly covered (set operations UNION/INTERSECT/EXCEPT, conditional logic CASE WHEN, date/time functions, string functions, query planning/index basics beyond a mention, data modeling/normalization), so not full marks. Python/Pandas/NumPy content is largely missing from the provided excerpt beyond a Week 2 objective and Day 8 heading, so core operations like merge/join, groupby/pivot, apply/vectorization, datetime handling, missing data strategies, and NumPy broadcasting cannot be confirmed. Visualization/statistics and ML weeks are not shown in the excerpt at all, so coverage cannot be verified; strict scoring assigns 0 for those sections.

2. Difficulty Progression Reasonableness.2a_structure_detection: 5/5 — has complete week division (13 weeks), detected 30 distinct Day(s)
2. Difficulty Progression Reasonableness.2b_comment: Starting point is mostly appropriate: beginning with SQL fundamentals fits an intermediate Python learner targeting data analyst roles, and Week 1 quickly ramps to joins/aggregations/subqueries/window functions (good relevance, slightly heavy for a single week at 2–3h/day). Weekly progression matches the recommended route (SQL -> Pandas/NumPy -> Viz/Stats -> ML) and is logically ordered; slight risk that ML in Week 4 compresses too much after only ~3 weeks of prep. Within Week 1, daily sequencing is coherent (SELECT/filtering -> joins -> group by -> subqueries/CTEs -> windows -> practice/project), though Day 7 requiring 10 HackerRank medium SQL plus a business report is likely overload. Comprehensive project planning is unclear in the provided content: there is a Week 1 mini-project (Chinook), but no explicit end-of-plan end-to-end capstone is visible in the excerpt; if Week 4 includes a true E2E project it should be clearly specified (dataset, deliverables, evaluation rubric).

3. Resource Authenticity & Quality (20 p.3a_quality_platforms: leetcode.com, matplotlib.org, mode.com, numpy.org, pandas.pydata.org, scikit-learn.org, seaborn.pydata.org, sqlbolt.com, www.coursera.org, www.datacamp.com, www.geeksforgeeks.org, www.hackerrank.com, www.kaggle.com, www.khanacademy.org, www.w3schools.com, www.youtube.com
3. Resource Authenticity & Quality (20 p.3b_comment: Authenticity is strong: the plan mostly references well-known, real platforms (freeCodeCamp YouTube, W3Schools, SQLBolt, SQLZoo, Mode Analytics, LeetCode, HackerRank, PostgreSQL docs, GeeksforGeeks, Chinook sample DB). Specificity is generally good (often includes exact lesson numbers and/or durations), but there are a few weaker/less precise items (e.g., “W3Schools SQL Advanced Tutorial” is vague, and “LeetCode Database Problems (Easy level, 3-5 problems)” isn’t a specific set). Resource-type diversity is excellent, combining videos, readings/docs, interactive lessons, coding challenge sites, and a real dataset/project.

4. Practicality & Executability (15 pts).4b_comment:
Daily practice tasks are clearly listed and specific for Days 1-7 (SQLBolt/SQLZoo/LeetCode/HackerRank + mini exercises + challenges), which is strong. However, the provided plan text cuts off at Day 8 and does not show whether Weeks 2-4 maintain the same daily practice structure; this uncertainty prevents a higher score given the requirement to score strictly (risk of missing/underspecified tasks later).

Time feasibility: Most days are estimated at 2.5 hours, matching the 2–3 hour constraint. Day 1 includes a 3.5-hour video plus additional reading/interactive practice, which is overloaded unless the video is partially deferred/skipped. Day 7 packs 10 medium HackerRank problems plus a multi-part Chinook business report into 3 hours—likely too tight for an intermediate learner if outputs are expected to be interview-ready.

Project quality: The Week 1 Chinook mini-project uses a real dataset and is practical for SQL. But the plan excerpt does not show any Week 4 end-to-end comprehensive project, nor clear integration of SQL + Python + visualization + statistics in a single capstone using a real-world dataset (e.g., Kaggle). With only the Week 1 SQL report visible, this scores low.

5. Document Format & Extras (10 pts).5b_structure_completeness: 4/4 — User profile:Y 4-week division:Y(13 weeks) Appendix/Tips:Y Daily time:Y
